It does not address DVS-PEI encapsulants, flexible electronics, or damp heat aging conditions (85C/85%RH). No extractable facts relevant to the research question."}, {"source_url": "https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC8747404/", "source_title": "Effect of Thermal Aging on Mechanical Properties and Color Difference of Glass Fiber Reinforced Polyetherimide Composites", "source_type": "journal_article", "source_quality": 0.75, "overall_relevance": 0.55, "year": 2022, "authors": ["Sun Y.", "Deng J.", "Xie Z.", "Nie Y.", "Liu Z."], "venue": "Polymers (MDPI)", "doi": "", "atomic_facts": [{"statement": "Tensile strength of 20% glass fiber reinforced PEI (GF/PEI) decreased from 73.7 MPa (unaged) to 58.2 MPa (aged at 145C for 180h), a 21% reduction with increasing aging temperature.", "direct_quote": "Tensile strength values of GF/PEI-0, GF/PEI-85, GF/PEI-100, GF/PEI-115, GF/PEI-130 and GF/PEI-145 were found as, 73.7, 73.3, 69.9, 67.2, 66.1 and 58.2 MPa, respectively.", "fact_category": "measurement", "relevance_score": 0.7, "confidence": 0.95, "perspective": "Scientific", "entities": ["GF/PEI", "polyetherimide", "tensile strength", "thermal aging"]}, {"statement": "The elastic modulus of GF/PEI composites remained approximately 2 GPa regardless of thermal aging temperature (85-145C), indicating aging does not affect stiffness.", "direct_quote": "the elastic modulus seems independent on the aging temperature, all the samples exhibited an elastic modulus of approximately 2 GPa", "fact_category": "measurement", "relevance_score": 0.55, "confidence": 0.95, "perspective": "Scientific", "entities": ["GF/PEI", "elastic modulus", "thermal aging"]}, {"statement": "SEM observation showed smooth glass fiber surfaces in GF/PEI-145 specimens, indicating obvious separation between glass fiber and PEI matrix at high aging temperatures, confirming interfacial adhesion degradation as the primary failure mechanism.", "direct_quote": "the smooth surface of glass fibers is observed in the GF/PEI-145 composite...there is obvious separation between glass fiber and matrix in the GF/PEI composites aged at high temperature.", "fact_category": "causal_link", "relevance_score": 0.75, "confidence": 0.9, "perspective": "Scientific", "entities": ["GF/PEI", "interfacial adhesion", "SEM", "fiber-matrix debonding"]}, {"statement": "Thermal aging of GF/PEI at 85-145C for 180h caused tensile strain to decrease from 4.6 +/- 0.5% to 3.3 +/- 0.3%, and fractured surfaces exhibited brittle fracture features without significant plastic deformation.", "direct_quote": "The tensile strain value of the composite composites gradually decreases from 4.6 +/- 0.5% to 3.3 +/- 0.3% with increasing the aging temperature.", "fact_category": "measurement", "relevance_score": 0.6, "confidence": 0.95, "perspective": "Scientific", "entities": ["GF/PEI", "tensile strain", "ductility", "brittle fracture"]}], "evidence_summary": "Study of thermal aging (85-145C, 180h, dry heat in air) on 20% glass fiber reinforced PEI. Tensile strength drops 21% (73.7 to 58.2 MPa), elastic modulus unchanged (~2 GPa), ductility decreases. SEM confirms fiber-matrix debonding. Relevant to PEI degradation under thermal aging but uses dry heat only (not damp heat 85C/85%RH) and studies GF/PEI composites rather than DVS-PEI encapsulants in flexible electronics."}]}
